name: Gangeshwar Mahadev Temple
state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
description: An ancient seaside cave temple where five Shiva lingas, believed to be established by the Pandavas, are naturally washed by the crashing waves of the Arabian Sea at high tide.
history: Legend states that the five Pandava brothers created these lingas during their exile. The continuous bathing of the lingas by the ocean makes it a unique spiritual site.
famousFor: Sea-washed Shiva lingas and Pandava legend.
